title: Nested zones, boundaries, and rendering quality
description: Complete nested zone/boundary rendering and dense-diagram quality work: zone hierarchy display, boundary nesting, overlay conflict priority, badge collapse/inspector handoff, edge/label legibility hardening, and acceptance examples.
triage_notes: IN PROGRESS. Cut from product principles rendering quality plus 03 zone view/nesting and overlay readability sections. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: fixed observed comprehensive-sample label overlap where zone labels (e.g. OnPrem/On-premises) collided with member nodes by reserving 36px top header padding for zone/boundary boxes and widening lane gap to keep adjacent boxes separated. Added layout regression test for zone label header clearance. Actual comprehensive sample OnPrem headerGap now 36px. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: added connection-density-aware component sizing in render layout. Node size now grows from graph edge degree plus permission overlay relationships so high-degree hubs get more internal padding and more port distribution room while low-degree nodes stay compact. Actual comprehensive sample CloudRun is now rendered as the largest hub size while GKECluster/Worker remain compact. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: changed permission overlay rendering from center-to-center straight lines to side-aware orthogonal paths with distributed source/target ports and stacked target-side labels. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: dense permission overlays now collapse long role labels and role badges into short in-node permission summaries; actual comprehensive sample permission overlay has 0 rendered roles/... texts and 9 permission summaries. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: normal graph edges incident to high-degree components now use balanced side-aware routing instead of concentrating on one or two faces; actual comprehensive sample CloudRun edges now use left 4 / right 3 / top 4 / bottom 4 endpoints. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: reciprocal/multi-edge pairs between the same two components now route on separate outside tracks instead of reusing the same line; Web->FirebaseAuth uses the upper track while FirebaseAuth->Web uses the lower track in the human-provided LR sample. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: viewer/playground settings now use the product taxonomy Views (overview/zone/layer), Render modes (2d/isometric/3d), and Overlays (auth/dataflow/boundary/permission/validation). Added layer as a registered semantic 2D view using layer-ranked layout; render-mode is accepted by render() and <archmap-viewer>. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: renderMode=isometric now routes to the optional Three.js renderer instead of static SVG, using an OrthographicCamera plus OrbitControls so the station-map/isometric look can be rotated, panned, and zoomed. 3D styling remains matched to the softer station-map palette: pastel layer colors, softer zone volumes, white background, pale floor plane/grid, and an isometric initial camera. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: clarified overlay checkbox semantics as additive information layers. Overview remains pure structure until overlays are checked; viewer controls now say Add info and call addOverlay/removeOverlay rather than blind toggle. Added regression coverage for overview structural baseline plus additive auth/dataflow layers. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: routing now preserves horizontal/vertical segments wherever possible and only allows diagonal middle segments for reciprocal edges or overcrowded node faces (4+ ports). Component connectors still always use horizontal/vertical first and last stubs; high-degree nodes can grow to 420x128; edge labels choose from wide candidate positions and avoid node boxes, prior labels, and edge segments. Actual comprehensive sample: CloudRun 15/15 incident edges have horizontal/vertical start and end stubs, 12/15 use diagonal middles due to overcrowded faces; label-vs-node hits 0; label-vs-label hits 0. Completion: nested modeled zones/boundaries render legibly; dense diagrams remain inspectable; no edge-over-node/shared-endpoint regressions in tests/examples. Remaining: nested zone hierarchy display, boundary nesting quality, selected highlight, diagnostics-to-diagram focus, broader non-permission edge-label collision handling, optional richer isometric/3D controls (angle presets/depth/layer visibility).
